review.correctedDescriptionShort: Preview of the Richmond Sockeyes' 1987-88 BCJHL season, highlighting their Centennial Cup title, returning players including goalie Glenn Pencer and scorer Rob Clarke, and coach Orland Kurtenbach's recruitment approach.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A Chilliwack Progress column reprinted in The Abbotsford News previews the 1987-88 BCJHL season with a focus on the Richmond Sockeyes, who won the Centennial Cup in spring 1987. Only four players return from the championship roster — none on defence — including five-year veteran goalie Glenn Pencer (4.20 GAA, shared duties with Frank Romeo), leading scorer Rob Clarke (65 goals, 35 assists, third in Coast Division), centre Jason Tallo, and right winger Tony Bobbit. Despite the thin returning core, coach Orland Kurtenbach's strong recruiting record is cited as the reason the Sockeyes are expected to remain competitive in the Coastal Division.
review.notes: No formal headline appears in the OCR for the Sockeyes section; it is a subsection of a broader BCJHL league preview. Byline corrected to include the originating paper (The Chilliwack Progress) as stated in the OCR. The corrected long description adds the Frank Romeo / GAA detail and the no-returning-defencemen nuance.
